title: BTC vs Ethereum to XMR: Which Crypto Swap Route Leaks Less, Costs Less, and Settles More Smoothly?
keyQuestions: Is BTC or Ethereum better if I want to swap into XMR?
Which route leaks less before I even receive Monero?
Does BTC to XMR usually feel simpler than Ethereum to XMR?
Does BTC to XMR cost less than Ethereum to XMR in real life?
Is Ethereum more exposed because my whole wallet history is public?
What is the smartest way to choose between BTC and Ethereum for an XMR swap?
quickSteps: Start with the asset you already hold if that avoids unnecessary extra conversions.
Compare the full route, not just the displayed rate.
Treat Ethereum as the more context-heavy path when the wallet has a long public history.
Do not add a middle step just because it sounds smarter.
Pick the route you are least likely to misuse.

recoveryDecisionTree.title: How to Choose Between BTC and Ethereum for an XMR Swap
recoveryDecisionTree.goal: Help the user choose the cleaner route into XMR with fewer leaks, lower practical overhead, and smoother execution.

branches.if: You already hold Bitcoin in a simple wallet setup
then: Compare the live BTC to XMR route first
Check total overhead instead of looking only at the headline rate
Use the simpler path if it avoids extra conversions
Keep the send side clean and avoid unnecessary extra steps

branches.if: You already hold Ethereum and want to avoid building a two-step route
then: Check the direct Ethereum to XMR route
Make sure the wallet setup is clear and ready
Remember that the sending side may carry more public account context
Prefer one clean direct move over two extra conversions unless there is a real reason

branches.if: You are unsure which route is cleaner at your amount
then: Compare fee overhead, confirmation flow, and operational complexity
Look at how much visible baggage the sending side already carries
Avoid adding a middle asset just because it sounds clever
Choose the route you are least likely to misuse

riskNotes: Neither BTC to XMR nor Ethereum to XMR is automatically private before the swap starts.
Ethereum often carries denser public wallet context because one address may expose a longer activity trail.
Bitcoin often feels simpler operationally, but simple does not mean invisible.
More steps usually mean more cost, more history, and more room for mistakes.
The displayed rate is only part of the real route cost.
A cleaner route is often the one with fewer moving parts and fewer assumptions.
